title: Image 19 of The Washington times (Washington [D.C.]), November 29, 1935
raw_ocr: MAXWELL GIRL SENTENCE IS DEFENDED WISE Va Nov 29 1N5 111feeling against what they de scribed as outsiders attempting to Interfere with their Jurisprudence was spreading among the elder hill folk today Hostility against the protests made by persons In other States against the 25year imprisonment imposed upon Edith Maxwell 21 for killing her father Trigg Max well was so pronounced that Edith was not allowed to see her mother yesterday Jim Dotson the jailer is Ediths uncle He said he refused the mother permission to visit Edith because I dont want to take any chances of increasing the bad feel ing among some of the folks here Because of this resentment among a certain class of her own people Edith had to eat her Thanksgiving dinner alone Her mother sent her a roast chicken vegetables and an angel food cake The mother faces trial next spring on a charge of being an accessory in the murder Meanwhile womens clubs in Virginia and in Tennessee ap pointed committees to come here and investigate Ediths case and seek to have the Jurys manda tory sentence of 25 years set aside either by a new trial or a Governors pardon Arguments for
